title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Public Schools faces job cuts with over 560 positions eliminated due to declining enrollment and budget constraints.
Mayor Wu's administration refuses to use reserve funds, citing long-term financial stability and immediate community needs.
Efforts continue to place affected educators into new positions, with many already reassigned or in the process.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Harmful bacteria remain in the Merrimack River after a sewer break in Haverhill contaminated the water.
Four out of seven testing sites had elevated fecal coliform and E. coli levels, impacting recreational activities.
Emergency shellfish closures are in effect in multiple North Shore areas, with testing ongoing to ensure safety.

title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: One man, Vilmar Dorner, remains missing after a boat crash off Salisbury Beach on Friday night.
Two individuals were rescued and hospitalized following the crash near Salisbury Beach State Reservation at 10:35 p.m.
Search efforts by Massachusetts State Police and U.S. Coast Guard were unsuccessful; the search was suspended Saturday evening.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in Nantucket on July 5, shortly after officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.
Sandler contacted the Nantucket Police non-emergency line to find a basketball game, as advised by dispatcher Chris Reynolds.
Players from the game praised Sandler’s elite passing skills and his approachable demeanor during the casual match.

title: Local obituary: Oliver Lewis, 15, ‘an old soul, with wisdom far beyond his years’
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, of Franklin, MA, passed away on June 23, 2026, from a bicycle accident.
Oliver was known for his kindness and passion for knowledge, excelling in both academics and sports.
In memory of Oliver, his family established a scholarship fund at Bishop Feehan High School.

title: Poll: Most in Mass. think police hid evidence in Karen Read case
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A UNH poll shows 69% of Massachusetts residents believe police concealed evidence in the Karen Read case.
Karen Read was acquitted of murder charges for the 2022 death of her boyfriend, officer John O’Keefe, yet faces ongoing lawsuits.
The poll revealed that nearly two-thirds of respondents view the case as indicative of broader policing issues in Massachusetts.

title: Duxbury man drowns while swimming to his boat
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: James Merlin, 66, drowned in Duxbury Bay after swimming to his boat, officials confirmed.
Merlin was unresponsive when bystanders brought him to shore; efforts to revive him were unsuccessful.
Plymouth County authorities do not suspect foul play; an investigation by state police is ongoing.

title: Groveland ice cream shop goes viral with scoop-tossing video
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A playful video from Jeff and Maria's Ice Cream shop in Groveland has amassed nearly 23 million Instagram views.
Jeff Boragine continues to run the ice cream shop in memory of his late wife, Maria, who passed away in 2018.
The shop is known for its fun social media videos, attracting visitors from afar to partake in the quirky experience.

title: Celtics sign first-round pick Chris Cenac Jr.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics have signed 19-year-old rookie power forward Chris Cenac Jr., the No. 27 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Despite being a developmental project, Cenac could address Boston's power forward needs with his 6'11" athletic frame.
Cenac played one season at Houston, helping them reach the Sweet 16, and averaged 9.5 points and 7.9 rebounds.

title: Local obituary: Riley J. Sergi, Central Catholic graduate from Dracut
author: Boston.com
bullets: Riley J. Sergi, 28, from Dracut, died on June 28, 2026, following injuries from a car accident.
Sergi was pursuing an electrician career at Whittier Tech and previously graduated from Central Catholic High School.
He is survived by his family in Haverhill and Billerica; donations are suggested to the Roadway Safety Foundation.
